title: Modular Desk Base
summary: A plywood cabinet base you size to your own desk — open shelves, a door, or drawers. The dimensions here are the standard recipe; adjust freely.
description:
This isn't one desk, it's a system: a plywood carcass sized to sit under a countertop at desk height, which you can build as an open cubby, a door cabinet, or a drawer bank, and gang side by side for a built-in. The numbers in this plan are the standard recommendation — 28-1/2" tall for a 30" finished desk height once you add a top, 16" wide, 24" deep — but the whole point is that you change them to fit your wall.

Two details keep a plywood cabinet looking built rather than boxy. First, iron-on edge banding on every exposed plywood edge — raw plywood edge grain is the tell that gives away a shop project, and banding hides it in minutes. Second, the base: hold the carcass up off the floor with a toe-kick sized to your baseboard (5-1/4" here) so it reads like cabinetry, not a box on the ground.

Bay supports do double duty — they tie the cabinet together and let you use less plywood, which is where most of the cost lives. A 1x3 or 1x4 at the top and a visible 1x6 across the bottom front are enough. Keep shelf spans at or under about 36" or they'll sag under load over time.
timeLabel: 5–10 hrs per base, more with doors/drawers

tools.note: Carcass shelves attach to the sides with pocket screws.
Ripping a full sheet into clean, straight 24"-deep strips. A track saw or circular saw with a guide also works.
Alternative to the table saw for breaking down the sheet.
Square crosscuts on the carcass pieces and supports.
Iron-on veneer edge banding hides the raw plywood edges — the difference between furniture and a box.

materials.name: 3/4" hardwood plywood
materials.note: Amount depends on how many bases you build; one sheet does a base or two.

materials.name: 1x3 or 1x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Top bay supports — furring strips are fine.

materials.name: Cabinet door with full-overlay euro hinges
materials.note: Optional — order or build. Height 1/2" less than the opening, width 1/2" less than the cabinet outside width.

materials.name: Drawer boxes, slides, and faces
materials.note: Optional. 20" or 22" slides; size faces to off-the-shelf 1x6/1x12 widths.

cutList.note: 24" is the depth rip; 28.5" gives a 30" finished desk height with a top.
Length = 16" outside width minus two 3/4" sides. Add shelves as your design needs; keep spans ≤ ~36".
Equal in length to the shelves.
On the outside, full cabinet width; measure and cut to fit — it's visible from the front.

steps.title: Decide your dimensions and cut the carcass
steps.body: Settle on your sizes before cutting. The standard recipe is 28-1/2" tall (for a 30" desk once a top is added), 16" wide, and a 24" deep rip; width can go up to about 36" before shelves start to sag. Rip the sheet into 24"-deep strips, then crosscut the sides and shelves to length.

steps.title: Band the exposed edges
steps.body: Iron edge banding onto every plywood edge that will show, trim it flush, and sand lightly. Doing this now, on flat parts, is far easier than after assembly — and it's the single step that makes plywood look like furniture.

steps.title: Assemble the carcass
steps.body:
Attach the shelves to the sides with four 3/4" pocket holes on each end and 1-1/4" pocket screws. Leave a 5-1/4" gap underneath for the base moulding (or adjust to your baseboard height). Every base needs at least the bottom shelf; add optional shelves at any height.

For a pull-out printer shelf, cut a plywood shelf 1" narrower than the interior and mount it on a euro drawer slide.

steps.title: Add the bay supports
steps.body: Tie everything together with bay supports — a 1x3 or 1x4 at the top and a 1x6 across the bottom front. The inside top supports equal the shelf length. The front 1x6 sits on the outside and is the full cabinet width, so measure and cut it to fit; it's visible, so treat it as a finished face. Screw or nail and glue in place.

steps.title: Add a door or drawers (optional), the base, and finish
steps.body:
If adding a door, size it 1/2" smaller than the opening in both height and width and hang it on full-overlay concealed hinges. If adding drawers, use 20" or 22" slides and size the faces to off-the-shelf 1x6/1x12 widths so you don't have to rip drawer fronts.

Wrap the bottom with 5-1/4" baseboard as a toe-kick, then prime and paint. Add your countertop last to reach desk height.
materials: Cabinet door with full-overlay euro hinges

images.alt: A built-in desk made from modular plywood cabinet bases with a countertop
